info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: A physician is ordering a thick blood film for a patient with suspected lymphatic filariasis caused by *Wuchereria bancrofti*. At what time should the blood specimen be collected to maximize diagnostic sensitivity?

options.label: During the onset of a high fever
Mid-day when the patient is most active
Late afternoon during the peak of symptoms
At night due to the periodicity of the microfilariae

q2.prompt: A 24-year-old woman presents with a profuse, frothy, yellow-green vaginal discharge and intense itching; physical examination reveals punctate hemorrhages on the cervix ("strawberry cervix"). Which organism is responsible for these findings?

q3.prompt: A public health official is investigating a food-borne outbreak of Cyclospora cayetanensis linked to imported raspberries. Why is direct person-to-person transmission via fresh fecal matter not observed with this parasite?

options.label: Oocysts are only produced during the sexual cycle in feline hosts
Oocysts must mature and sporulate in the environment to become infectious
Parasites require an intermediate snail host for larval development
The infectious stage is a fragile trophozoite that dies outside the host
Trophozoites must encyst within the gallbladder before being passed

q4.prompt: A 34-year-old male returns from a tropical region and presents with periodic fever paroxysms. A Giemsa-stained thin blood smear is performed, revealing enlarged, pale-staining infected erythrocytes that are distinctly oval in shape with characteristic fimbriated (jagged) edges. James'/Schüffner's dots are clearly visible within the host cell cytoplasm. Which of the following Plasmodium species is the most likely causative agent?

q5.prompt: A 42-year-old patient with chronic Schistosoma mansoni infection presents with hematemesis and splenomegaly; clinical evaluation confirms the presence of portal hypertension and periportal fibrosis. Which event in the parasite's life cycle is the primary cause of these manifestations?

options.label: Attachment of adult worms to the bladder wall
Deposition of eggs in the mesenteric vessels and liver
Direct invasion of the spleen by cercariae
Migration of miracidia through the pulmonary capillaries
Multiplication of schizonts within the hepatocytes

q6.prompt: A patient being treated for a Bacteroides fragilis infection is prescribed Metronidazole; two days later, after consuming a single beer, they develop severe facial flushing, tachycardia, and nausea. What is the mechanism behind this adverse reaction?

options.label: Accumulation of acetaldehyde due to enzyme inhibition
Hypersensitivity reaction to the nitroimidazole ring
Induction of the cytochrome P-450 system
Irreversible inhibition of the DNA synthesis complex
Massive release of toxins from killed anaerobic bacteria

q7.prompt: A patient with chronic malabsorption and fatty stools undergoes a string test (Entero-Test); microscopy of the bile-stained mucus reveals pear-shaped, flagellated trophozoites containing two nuclei. Which parasite is the most likely cause?

q8.prompt: A physician in an endemic region is screening patients for lymphatic filariasis using a thick peripheral blood film. Which diagnostic stage is the technologist looking for under the microscope?

q9.prompt: A 70-year-old man who lived in an endemic region decades ago is prescribed high-dose corticosteroids for an autoimmune condition; shortly after, he develops a hyperinfection syndrome with disseminated larvae. Which mechanism allowed this parasite to persist within the host for such a long duration?

options.label: Formation of calcified hydatid cysts in the liver
Sequestration of bradyzoites in skeletal muscle
Survival of adult worms in the mesenteric veins
Transformation of eggs into dormant hypnozoites

q10.prompt: A community's municipal water supply is found to be contaminated with protozoal cysts. Which of the following interventions is most effective for rendering the water safe for consumption given that standard chlorination is insufficient?
